title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: This test plan targets the top-level validation of CCF (Central Control Fabric) power management (PM) flows within the SOC. It covers coordinated power state transitions, resource arbitration, stress detection and response, license handshake and arbitration, policy-driven flow control, and system-level observability and debug. CCF integrates with PMA, Punit, and core modules to orchestrate power state changes (C-states, frequency/voltage scaling), manage exclusive access to shared resources (MLC flush, IOSF channels), and enforce system policies. Key interactions include cross-module synchronization, telemetry-driven stress handling, license negotiation, and debug visibility, ensuring robust and safe PM flow operation under normal and stress conditions.

title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: This test plan encompasses validation of: (1) coordinated state transitions and resource arbitration across cores/modules, including exclusive access scenarios; (2) telemetry and AI-based stress detection and response mechanisms for electrical, thermal, and power virus events; (3) license handshake and arbitration flows between PMA, Punit, and cores; (4) policy-driven flow control and ownership prioritization; (5) system-level observability and debug mechanisms for PM flows, stress events, and arbitration outcomes. The plan addresses normal operation, stress scenarios, contention, race conditions, and system recovery. It does not cover lower-level signal integrity or individual IP-specific PM flows outside CCF orchestration.

title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: All features require access to SOC-level debug and telemetry infrastructure, programmable stress injection capabilities, and synchronized PMA/Punit/core interfaces. Test environment must support controlled stress event generation, license arbitration triggers, and policy configuration. Observability tools must be available for real-time status monitoring and debug.

title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: Potential security implications include unauthorized PM flow initiation, license spoofing, and denial-of-service via resource contention. Mitigations involve policy gating, license arbitration, and observability mechanisms to detect and prevent malicious or unintended transitions. Validation will ensure that security/performance policies are enforced and that flow ownership is correctly prioritized under stress and error conditions.

title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: Risks include incomplete coverage of simultaneous stress events, false positives in stress detection, and loss of debug visibility under high contention. Contingencies involve fallback flows, enhanced observability, and additional stress scenario coverage to ensure robustness and survivability.

title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: Prerequisites include validated PMA/Punit/core interfaces, functional telemetry and debug infrastructure, and programmable policy and license configuration. Stress injection tools and observability mechanisms must be operational prior to test execution.

title: Coordinated State Transitions and Resource Arbitration
subsection content: Validate that CCF correctly staggers and synchronizes power state transitions (C6/MC6, frequency/voltage changes) across cores/modules, including exclusive access scenarios (MLC flush, IOSF channel arbitration). Check for correct handling of race conditions, contention, and out-of-sync status updates under normal and stress conditions.

title: Coordinated MC6 Entry and MLC Flush with Race/Abort Handling
subsection content: Validate correct MC6 entry and MLC flush coordination, including abort handling during wake-up races.

title: Coordinated MC6 Entry and MLC Flush with Race/Abort Handling
subsection content: Randomly trigger C6 entry on both cores, inject wake-up/abort events during MLC flush, and monitor state transitions.

title: Coordinated MC6 Entry and MLC Flush with Race/Abort Handling
subsection content: Randomize C6 entry timing offsets, wake-up/abort event injection, and flush progress to create contention and race conditions.

title: Coordinated MC6 Entry and MLC Flush with Race/Abort Handling
subsection content: Both cores active, system configured for independent C6 entry, PMA/MLC signal sampling, PM/MLC abort indication handling.

title: Coordinated MC6 Entry and MLC Flush with Race/Abort Handling
subsection content: Monitor MC3/MC6 state transitions, flush start/abort/done signals, and check for deadlocks or out-of-sync states.

title: Coordinated MC6 Entry and MLC Flush with Race/Abort Handling
subsection content: PM, MLC, and core state machines interact during C6/MC3/MC6 transitions and flush/abort flows.

title: Coordinated MC6 Entry and MLC Flush with Race/Abort Handling
subsection content: Observability of PMA/MLC signals, flush abort indication, and flush-done signal; check for deadlocks and state sync.

title: Simultaneous C6 Entry and MLC Flush Race Handling
subsection content: Configure both cores to independently request C6 entry. Randomly trigger C6 entry on both cores with timing offsets to create contention. During MC3, inject wake-up or abort requests while MLC flush is in progress. Monitor PMA/MLC signals for flush start, abort, and flush done. Check that flush is aborted and cores exit C6 promptly if a wake-up occurs.

title: Simultaneous C6 Entry and MLC Flush Race Handling
subsection content: 1. Enable both cores and configure for independent C6 entry. 2. Randomly trigger C6 entry on both cores with timing offsets. 3. Once both cores are in C6 (MC3), initiate MLC flush. 4. During flush, inject wake-up or abort requests to one or both cores. 5. Sample PMA/MLC signals for flush start, abort, and flush done. 6. Monitor MC3/MC6 state transitions. 7. Check for correct handling of abort and prompt C6 exit.

title: Simultaneous C6 Entry and MLC Flush Race Handling
subsection content: Pass if flush is aborted upon wake-up, cores exit C6 promptly, and no deadlocks or out-of-sync states occur. Fail if flush continues after wake-up, deadlock, or incorrect state transition is observed.

title: Simultaneous C6 Entry and MLC Flush Race Handling
subsection content: Use PMON, logs, and Visa to confirm flush abort, C6 exit, and state transitions.

title: Simultaneous C6 Entry and MLC Flush Race Handling
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. PVT checkers for flush abort and MC6 entry.

title: Simultaneous C6 Entry and MLC Flush Race Handling
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS latest, PMON v3.2, Perspec path, PythonSV, Supercollider, Prime95 for stress, register checks, concurrency utilities.

title: Simultaneous C6 Entry and MLC Flush Race Handling
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS v2.1, C6/MC6 knobs enabled, OS kernel 6.1, scheduler default, PCIe/storage/graphics drivers latest, registry keys for power state overrides.

title: Simultaneous C6 Entry and MLC Flush Race Handling
subsection title: Test HW Requirements and HW Configuration *
subsection content: SoC stepping B0, TDP 95W, dual-socket platform, 4x DIMM DDR5, PCIe Gen4 GPU, thermal solution: active heatsink, debug instrumentation: PMA/MLC signal probes, power measurement: external meters.

title: MLC Flush Abort During MC3 with Wake-Up Injection
subsection content: During MC3 (all cores in C6, MLC not yet flushed), initiate MLC flush and inject wake-up events to one or both cores. Observe PM sending abort indication to MLC and MLC returning flush-done signal. Verify that flush is stopped and wake-up proceeds without deadlock.

title: MLC Flush Abort During MC3 with Wake-Up Injection
subsection content: 1. Set both cores to C6, enter MC3. 2. Start MLC flush. 3. Inject wake-up event to one core during flush. 4. PM sends abort indication to MLC. 5. MLC returns flush-done signal. 6. Monitor core exit from C6 and MC3. 7. Check for absence of deadlock and correct state.

title: MLC Flush Abort During MC3 with Wake-Up Injection
subsection content: Pass if flush is aborted, flush-done signal is returned, and cores exit C6 without deadlock. Fail if flush is not aborted, deadlock, or incorrect state occurs.

title: MLC Flush Abort During MC3 with Wake-Up Injection
subsection content: Visa, PMON, and logs to confirm abort indication, flush-done signal, and C6 exit.

title: MLC Flush Abort During MC3 with Wake-Up Injection
subsection content: SST, Central, Concurrency checkers enabled. PVT checker for abort handling.

title: MLC Flush Abort During MC3 with Wake-Up Injection
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS v2.0, PMON v3.2, Perspec path, PythonSV, Supercollider, Prime95, concurrency and register check utilities.

title: MLC Flush Abort During MC3 with Wake-Up Injection
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS v2.1, C6/MC6 knobs, OS kernel 6.1, PCIe/storage/graphics drivers latest, registry keys for power state overrides.

title: MC6 Entry Only After Successful MLC Flush Completion
subsection content: Validate that MC6 is only entered after successful MLC flush completion. Randomly trigger C6 entry and monitor flush progress. Ensure MC6 entry is blocked if flush is aborted and only allowed if flush completes without abort.

title: MC6 Entry Only After Successful MLC Flush Completion
subsection content: 1. Enable both cores, configure for independent C6 entry. 2. Randomly trigger C6 entry, enter MC3. 3. Start MLC flush. 4. Monitor for wake-up/abort events. 5. If flush completes, allow MC6 entry. 6. If flush is aborted, block MC6 entry and exit C6. 7. Sample PMA/MLC signals and state transitions.

title: MC6 Entry Only After Successful MLC Flush Completion
subsection content: Pass if MC6 entry occurs only after flush completion, and is blocked if flush is aborted. Fail if MC6 entry occurs after abort or incorrect state transition.

title: MC6 Entry Only After Successful MLC Flush Completion
subsection content: PMON, logs, Visa to confirm MC6 entry only after flush completion.

subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS v2.1, C6/MC6 knobs, OS kernel 6.1, PCIe/storage/graphics drivers latest, registry keys for power state overrides.

title: ILP MEA Request with RLP in C6 State
subsection content: Randomly select ILP and RLP roles. ILP requests exclusive access (MEA) to perform MLC flush while RLP is in C6. ILP writes CORE_PMA_CR_UCODE_COMMAND[Prevent_other_core_wakeup] to block RLP wakeup. Monitor MEA grant and flush progress.

title: ILP MEA Request with RLP in C6 State
subsection content: 1. Enable both cores, assign ILP/RLP roles randomly. 2. Set RLP to C6 state. 3. ILP writes Prevent_other_core_wakeup in CORE_PMA_CR_UCODE_COMMAND. 4. ILP requests MEA and initiates MLC flush. 5. Monitor MEA grant, flush progress, and RLP wakeup blocking. 6. Release MEA after flush.

title: ILP MEA Request with RLP in C6 State
subsection content: Pass if MEA is granted only when RLP is in C6 and wakeup is blocked, flush proceeds, and MEA is released correctly. Fail if MEA is granted incorrectly, deadlock, or lost MEA request.

title: Race Condition Handling During MEA Arbitration
subsection content: Randomize MEA request timing and RLP state transitions to create race conditions. Monitor MEA arbitration, check for deadlocks, lost MEA requests, and correct MEA release after flush. Ensure only one bit is set in CORE_PMA_CR_UCODE_COMMAND at a time.

title: Race Condition Handling During MEA Arbitration
subsection content: 1. Enable both cores, assign ILP/RLP roles randomly. 2. Randomize MEA request timing and RLP state transitions (C6, C0, transitioning). 3. ILP requests MEA via Prevent_other_core_wakeup or IMI as appropriate. 4. Monitor MEA arbitration, flush progress, and MEA release. 5. Check that only one bit is set in CORE_PMA_CR_UCODE_COMMAND at any time. 6. Monitor for deadlocks or lost MEA requests.

title: Race Condition Handling During MEA Arbitration
subsection content: Pass if MEA arbitration resolves races correctly, no deadlocks or lost requests, and only one bit is set in register. Fail if deadlock, lost MEA request, or register misconfiguration occurs.

subsection content: PMON, logs, Visa to confirm race resolution, MEA grant, flush progress, and register state.

title: AGV/FGV Request Contention and Atomicity Validation
subsection content: Randomly trigger AGV/FGV requests on one or both cores, including during atomic flows (C6/FGV). Monitor GV_CTRL arbitration, workpoint application, and atomicity. Validate that transitions are atomic and no partial or out-of-sync WP application occurs.

title: AGV/FGV Request Contention and Atomicity Validation
subsection content: 1. Enable both cores, configure for AGV/FGV requests. 2. Randomly trigger AGV/FGV requests during atomic flows (C6/FGV). 3. Monitor GV_CTRL arbitration and workpoint application. 4. Check for atomicity and correct application. 5. Validate that no partial or out-of-sync WP application occurs.

title: AGV/FGV Request Contention and Atomicity Validation
subsection content: Pass if transitions are atomic, workpoints are applied correctly, and no partial or out-of-sync application occurs. Fail if atomicity is violated or incorrect WP application occurs.

subsection content: PMON, logs, Visa to confirm AGV/FGV arbitration, atomicity, and workpoint application.

subsection content: Both cores enabled, SBFT@F test requested, ucode_command[i].enter_wf_diag_sleep, Piclet activation, PMA_TRAP event monitoring.

title: SBFT@F Entry Coordination with PM Event Injection
subsection content: Randomly trigger SBFT@F entry on one or both cores. Inject PM events (interrupts, CPD) during entry to test arbitration and abort handling. Monitor WF_DIAG_SLEEP flag, PMA_TRAP events, and SBFT status. Validate that only one core initiates SBFT@F start and entry is aborted if PM event occurs before both cores are ready.

title: SBFT@F Entry Coordination with PM Event Injection
subsection content: 1. Enable both cores, request SBFT@F test. 2. Randomly trigger SBFT@F entry on one or both cores. 3. Set ucode_command[i].enter_wf_diag_sleep before activating Piclet. 4. Inject PM event during entry. 5. Monitor WF_DIAG_SLEEP flag, PMA_TRAP events, and SBFT status. 6. Check that entry is aborted if PM event occurs before both cores are ready. 7. Validate that only one core initiates SBFT@F start.

title: SBFT@F Entry Coordination with PM Event Injection
subsection content: Pass if SBFT@F entry is aborted upon PM event, only one core initiates start, and no deadlocks or lost events occur. Fail if entry proceeds after PM event, deadlock, or lost event.

title: SBFT@F Diagnostic Sleep Coordination and Release
subsection content: Validate correct coordination and release of SBFT@F diagnostic sleep. Both cores independently enter 'ready for SBFT@F' state. The last core to enter triggers module-level actions. Monitor WF_DIAG_SLEEP flag, SBFT status, and module-level actions. Ensure diagnostic sleep is correctly released.

title: SBFT@F Diagnostic Sleep Coordination and Release
subsection content: 1. Enable both cores, request SBFT@F test. 2. Both cores independently enter 'ready for SBFT@F' state. 3. Set ucode_command[i].enter_wf_diag_sleep before activating Piclet. 4. The last core to enter triggers module-level actions (C6-Save, MLC flush, SBFT@F program loading, MISR/uBp configs, U2A mailbox). 5. Monitor WF_DIAG_SLEEP flag, SBFT status, and module-level actions. 6. Validate correct release of diagnostic sleep.

title: SBFT@F Diagnostic Sleep Coordination and Release
subsection content: Pass if diagnostic sleep is coordinated and released correctly, module-level actions proceed, and no deadlocks or lost events occur. Fail if coordination or release is incorrect, deadlock, or lost event.

title: Mutual Exclusion of SBFT@F and Static Lockstep
subsection content: Validate that SBFT@F and static lockstep are mutually exclusive. Attempt to activate SBFT@F while module is configured in static lockstep. Monitor SBFT status and module configuration. Ensure SBFT@F is not entered if static lockstep is active.

title: Mutual Exclusion of SBFT@F and Static Lockstep
subsection content: 1. Enable both cores, configure module in static lockstep. 2. Attempt to activate SBFT@F. 3. Monitor SBFT status and module configuration. 4. Check that SBFT@F is not entered if static lockstep is active.

title: Mutual Exclusion of SBFT@F and Static Lockstep
subsection content: Pass if SBFT@F is not entered when static lockstep is active. Fail if SBFT@F entry occurs during static lockstep.
